text: Abendsin et al. (2018) JGR Atmos. The reactive partitioning of cis and trans β-IEPOX was studied on hydrated inorganic seed particles in the absence of acids. In spite of a liquid water content on the order of 40–75% relative to the total aerosol mass, no OA was produced on dry AS. In contrast, both cis and trans β-IEPOX gave rise very steeply to yield large amounts of OA on AS seeds. IEPOX OA formation is a kinetically limited process and therefore, if there is any IEPOX left in the gas phase, the OA growth will continue. We could not discern any differences in the amounts and compositions of the IEPOX-derived OA for the two isomers. Significant amounts of OA (equivalent to up to 85% of the seed mass in this case) were also produced on humidified AS seeds in the presence of base (NaOH). The pH dependence for IEPOX-derived OA formation therefore appears to be weak. After drying the seed particles, no OA was produced. For Na+ substituted for NH4+, no OA was formed. The Henry's Law partitioning to aqueous NaCl was measured to be 3 × 107 M atm−1 (−50 / +100%). A small amount of OA was produced in presence of NH4+ but with sulfate (SO42−) substituted with chloride (Cl−). Online TOF-AMS and offline filter data show the presence of oxygenated hydrocarbons, organosulfates and amines within the IEPOX-derived OA. The findings here are in agreement with the weak correlations observed between IEPOX-derived OA and particle acidity and liquid water content that were reported in several field studies. It was argued here that the IEPOX chemistry is nucleophile-limited.
